gotchas.title: Return null when no file change is needed
gotchas.summary: A JSSG transform should return null for non-applicable files instead of returning unchanged source text by default.
gotchas.content: Use an early null return when no matches or edits are needed. This is the normal way to indicate a skipped file.

gotchas.title: Commit collected edits from the root node
gotchas.summary: Apply deterministic edits with root.root().commitEdits(edits) or rootNode.commitEdits(edits) after collecting them.
gotchas.content: Collect edits first, then commit them from the root AST node instead of mutating source text directly.

gotchas.title: Update codemod.yaml when using gated runtime APIs
gotchas.summary: If a codemod uses gated APIs such as fs, fetch, or child_process, update codemod.yaml capabilities in the same change.
gotchas.content: Runtime-gated APIs must be paired with the matching capabilities entry in codemod.yaml; otherwise the package is incomplete.

gotchas.title: Treat metrics.json as part of expected test output
gotchas.summary: Metrics snapshots are handled separately from directory tree comparisons and must be kept in sync when used.
gotchas.content: If a package uses metrics snapshots, refresh or fix metrics.json along with the rest of the expected outputs.

recipes.title: Use directory fixtures when the codemod creates or deletes files
recipes.summary: For codemods that create preview routes, helpers, or multiple related file changes, use input/ and expected/ directory fixtures.
recipes.content: Single-file fixtures are not enough for file-creation or multi-file codemods. Use directory snapshots so the whole tree is asserted.

recipes.title: Use jssgTransform for related deterministic secondary files
recipes.summary: When a codemod needs to update a related file from the primary transform, prefer jssgTransform instead of a shell hop.
recipes.content: jssgTransform applies a transform to another file and collects those changes atomically with the primary file. This is preferred over shell-based coordination for deterministic related files.
